questions.q: What is the primary objective of Horizontal Analysis?
opts: (A) To compare the current year's financial position with previous years
(B) To compare the financial position of a company with its competitors
(C) To measure the profitability and efficiency of a company
(D) To compare the financial position of a company with its industry average
questions.explanation: Horizontal Analysis is used to compare the current year's financial position with previous years to identify trends and variations in financial performance.

questions.q: Which of the following is a limitation of Ratio Analysis?
opts: (A) It helps in identifying the strengths and weaknesses of a company
(B) It is not useful for comparing companies with different capital structures
(C) It provides a detailed analysis of a company's performance
(D) It is not useful for analyzing the financial position of a company at a particular point of time
questions.explanation: Ratio Analysis has its limitations, including the fact that it is not useful for comparing companies with different capital structures, as ratios can be affected by the capital structure.

questions.q: What is the purpose of Vertical Analysis?
opts: (A) To compare the financial position of a company with its industry average
(B) To measure the profitability and efficiency of a company
(C) To compare the current year's financial position with previous years
(D) To provide a detailed analysis of a company's performance
questions.explanation: Vertical Analysis is used to provide a detailed analysis of a company's performance by expressing the financial statements as a percentage of sales or another base.

questions.q: Which of the following ratios is used to measure the liquidity of a company?
opts: (A) Current Ratio
(B) Debt-Equity Ratio
(C) Return on Equity (ROE)
(D) Price-Earnings Ratio
questions.explanation: The Current Ratio is used to measure the liquidity of a company by comparing its current assets to its current liabilities.

questions.q: What is the purpose of Du Pont Analysis?
opts: (A) To measure the profitability of a company
(B) To measure the efficiency of a company
(C) To measure the liquidity of a company
(D) To measure the overall effectiveness of a company's operations
questions.explanation: Du Pont Analysis is used to measure the overall effectiveness of a company's operations by breaking down the Return on Equity (ROE) into its components, profitability and efficiency.

questions.q: Which of the following is a common limitation of Horizontal Analysis?
opts: (A) It is not useful for comparing companies with different capital structures
(B) It is not useful for analyzing the financial position of a company at a particular point of time
(C) It is not useful for identifying trends and variations in financial performance
(D) It is not useful for comparing the current year's financial position with previous years
questions.explanation: Horizontal Analysis has its limitations, including the fact that it is not useful for comparing the current year's financial position with previous years, as it may not capture the impact of changes in accounting policies or other factors.

questions.q: What is the purpose of Trend Analysis?
opts: (A) To compare the financial position of a company with its industry average
(B) To measure the profitability and efficiency of a company
(C) To compare the current year's financial position with previous years
(D) To provide a detailed analysis of a company's performance
questions.explanation: Trend Analysis is used to compare the current year's financial position with previous years to identify trends and variations in financial performance.

questions.q: Which of the following ratios is used to measure the efficiency of a company?
opts: (A) Current Ratio
(B) Debt-Equity Ratio
(C) Return on Equity (ROE)
(D) Asset Turnover Ratio
questions.explanation: The Asset Turnover Ratio is used to measure the efficiency of a company by comparing its sales to its assets.

opts: (A) Current Ratio
(B) Debt-Equity Ratio
(C) Return on Equity (ROE)
(D) Asset Turnover Ratio
questions.explanation: The Return on Equity (ROE) is used to measure the profitability of a company by comparing its net income to its shareholders' equity.

questions.q: Which of the following ratios is used to measure the liquidity of a company, excluding cash?
opts: (A) Current Ratio
(B) Debt-Equity Ratio

questions.q: Which of the following ratios is used to measure the efficiency of a company, excluding current assets?
opts: (A) Current Ratio
(B) Debt-Equity Ratio

questions.explanation: The Asset Turnover Ratio (excluding current assets) is used to measure the efficiency of a company, excluding current assets, by comparing its sales to its non-current assets.
